title: baccharis juncea
intro: Baccharis juncea, also known as tall groundsel, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its tall, erect stems and small, white or pink flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Baccharis juncea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for its medicinal purposes.
